sections.paragraph:
a root not used in Kal, which had the signification of turning, rolling, and hence of disturbing, perplexing, comp. אָבַךְ, أَفَكَ and הָפַךְ to turn, to turn about, Arab. بَاكَ to be confused, disturbed, used of any affair, نك to involve oneself in evil.

Niphal נָבֹוךְ Part. pl. נְבֻכִים ( Exo 14:3), to be entangled, Est 3:15 to wander in perplexity, Joe 1:18 Ex. loc. cit.; hence מְבוּכָה.
